mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate for equity cash flows, compressing forward S&P 500 valuations and generating negative 20-day returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC > 0.05 or positive returns dominate when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an imminent labor market contraction that reduces corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of triggered windows despite rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized demand shock across consumer and housing sectors, which transmits to corporate revenues and produces negative quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization that supports near-term earnings momentum, generating positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative during late-cycle expansions despite positive INDPRO growth.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 210 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises borrowing costs and suppresses equity valuations over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are non-negative in >70% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and stable unemployment rates indicates robust labor market absorption that sustains consumer spending power, driving positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint predictor fails to outperform a baseline model with a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic expansion that supports corporate profit margins, producing positive 5-day S&P 500 returns through earnings momentum.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between GDPC1 growth and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.02 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of industrial production contraction signal a supply-side bottleneck or demand withdrawal that compresses forward earnings estimates, generating negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in >60% of triggered instances despite consecutive industrial production declines.
